memo: The packet describes The AI Scientist-v2, an autonomous research pipeline with four implemented evaluation mechanisms. (1) A literature-review tool (Semantic Scholar) queried during idea formulation to assess novelty and surface prior work, feeding the decision to pursue a research avenue (E01). (2) A dedicated LLM evaluator that scores tree nodes on performance metrics, training dynamics, and plot quality; it both guides best-first expansion within a stage (E11) and selects the best-performing node carried forward as the root/seed of the next stage (E02, E10) — i.e., a single evaluator serves simultaneously as a diagnostic ranker and as a hard stage gate. (3) A VLM critique of generated visualizations, whose flags flip node status to buggy and whose feedback is stored for future debugging, so critique output routes both to the search policy (buggy nodes are preferentially expanded with a predefined probability, E11) and to the debugging context (E03, E10). (4) External blind peer review at the ICBINB workshop, with 3 of 43 submissions AI-generated and reviewers blinded to which; one of three cleared the acceptance threshold at 6.33/10 and two did not (E04, E09). Human evaluative judgment is retained at two boundary points: selecting which AI-generated ideas to run through the full pipeline, and selecting the single best manuscript per idea from multiple seed runs (E07, E08, E12). Architecturally the system moved away from fixed human-authored templates (E05) and from linear experimentation toward agentic tree search across all four stages (E06). Key exposures: the LLM node evaluator is an unvalidated single point of failure whose errors compound irreversibly across stage transitions (no calibration or held-out validation is reported); the strongest external signal — peer review — arrives after the run terminates and is never reported as routed back into the tree; and the reported outcome is confounded by two undocumented human selection filters, so end-to-end acceptance rate does not cleanly measure autonomous capability. Recommendations target evaluator validation, separating the diagnostic ranking role from the irreversible stage gate, and clarifying the human-in-the-loop system boundary for outcome claims.

diagnostics.statement: The LLM node evaluator performs two distinct functions — continuous best-first ranking within a stage and an irreversible selection of the single node seeding the next stage — with no reported calibration, held-out validation, or error analysis of its judgments.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: After each stage, the experiment manager selects the best-performing node using a dedicated LLM evaluator (see next section) based on clearly articulated criteria. This selected node is then carried forward to seed the subsequent experimentation stage.
A best-performing node, selected by LLM-based evaluation, is passed down as the root node of Stage 2.
the system uses a best-first search strategy, guided by an LLM that evaluates candidates based on factors like performance metrics, training dynamics, and the quality of generated plots

diagnostics.consequence: A single mis-ranking at a stage boundary discards the entire remaining tree and propagates unrecoverably into all downstream experimentation, analysis, and writing, with no mechanism reported to detect or reverse the error.

diagnostics.statement: VLM critique output is fed back into the search and debugging loop within a run, but the strongest external evaluation signal — blind workshop peer review with numeric scores — is reported only as a terminal outcome and is not reported as returning to any later system decision.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Any issues flagged by the VLM (such as unclear labels, missing legends, or misleading visualizations) result in the node being marked as buggy, and this feedback is recorded for future debugging.
Blind Peer Review: The three AI-generated submissions were included among the 43 total submissions received by the ICBINB workshop.
The remaining two submissions received lower scores and were not accepted.

diagnostics.consequence: The system optimizes against internal LLM/VLM proxies that are never checked against the external reviewer signal, so proxy-to-outcome divergence — including the two rejections — cannot be diagnosed or corrected in subsequent runs.

diagnostics.statement: The reported end-to-end result is produced under two human selection filters (choosing which AI ideas to fund and choosing the best manuscript per idea across multiple seed runs), so the boundary between autonomous system performance and human-curated performance is stated but not quantified.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: This initial idea selection step allowed us to manage computational resources by choosing which distinct, AI-generated starting points to explore further with the full system. It did not involve modifying the ideas themselves.
we selected the single best-resulting manuscript for submission based on a careful inspection of its overall coherence and scientific quality
the entire process within any single run—hypothesis refinement, code generation, execution, analysis, visualization, and writing—was performed autonomously

diagnostics.consequence: The 1-of-3 acceptance figure cannot be attributed to the autonomous pipeline alone; unreported counts of discarded ideas and manuscripts mean the true per-run success rate is unknown and the claim is not reproducible by a third party.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: After each stage, the experiment manager selects the best-performing node using a dedicated LLM evaluator (see next section) based on clearly articulated criteria.
the system uses a best-first search strategy, guided by an LLM that evaluates candidates based on factors like performance metrics, training dynamics, and the quality of generated plots

success_check.observable: Agreement between the LLM evaluator's node ranking at each stage boundary and an independent reference ranking (blinded human or metric-based ground truth) over a logged set of candidate nodes
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Report rank agreement and top-1 selection accuracy with confidence intervals; flag the evaluator as unfit for gating where top-1 accuracy is not distinguishable from a metrics-only baseline
success_check.evidence_needed: Logged per-node evaluator scores and criteria at each stage transition, plus independently produced reference rankings for the same nodes

exact_quotes.exact_quote: This selected node is then carried forward to seed the subsequent experimentation stage. The manager also records checkpoints at each stage’s completion.
Nodes that successfully execute and pass the VLM review without issue are designated as non-buggy.
A best-performing node, selected by LLM-based evaluation, is passed down as the root node of Stage 2.

success_check.observable: Whether LLM/VLM scores are used as advisory diagnostics feeding a separately specified gating rule (e.g., carrying forward k>1 seed nodes or requiring an execution-verified criterion) rather than the gate itself
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Number of nodes seeding the next stage is greater than 1, or the gate decision is reproducible from recorded non-LLM criteria in 100% of stage transitions
success_check.evidence_needed: Architecture specification and stage-transition logs showing the gating rule, seeds carried forward, and checkpoint contents

exact_quotes.exact_quote: we selected the three most promising initial ideas–two from the first batch, and one from the second batch–based on alignment with the workshop theme and potential interest
From the multiple complete manuscripts generated for each initial idea (i.e., one manuscript per seed), we selected the single best-resulting manuscript for submission
one manuscript achieved a sufficiently high average reviewer score (6.33 out of 10, with individual scores of 6, 6, and 7) to surpass the workshop’s acceptance threshold
humans initiated the process by providing the high-level workshop theme and selected which initial AI-generated ideas to run multiple times through the full pipeline

success_check.observable: A published boundary statement reporting the total ideas generated, ideas selected, seeds/manuscripts produced per idea, and manuscripts discarded, alongside the acceptance outcome
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: All four counts reported, enabling an unfiltered per-run acceptance rate to be computed and compared against the 1-of-3 submitted-manuscript figure
success_check.evidence_needed: Run-level inventory of generated ideas and manuscripts with the human selection criteria applied at each filter
